home.placeholder: Welcome to Shakh Clinics — your clinic in Moscow

items.q: How do I book an appointment at Shakh Clinics?
items.a: Fill in the short form on the website — our administrator will call you back within 15 minutes to confirm a convenient time. You can also reach us by phone around the clock.

items.q: Do you accept patients with medical insurance?
items.a: We work with most major insurance providers in Uzbekistan, including voluntary health insurance plans. Before your visit, please check with our administrator whether your service is covered — we will help you prepare the necessary documentation.

items.q: How long does an initial consultation last?
items.a: An initial appointment with a therapist or specialist typically lasts 30 to 40 minutes. This is enough time to take a full history, perform an examination, discuss your concerns, and outline a plan for further investigation or treatment.

items.q: Do I need a referral from another doctor?
items.a: No. You can book an appointment with any Shakh Clinics specialist directly, without a referral. If you already have records or test results from other clinics, please bring them along — this will help speed up diagnosis.

Yes. We have experienced pediatricians on staff and provide routine vaccinations following the Uzbekistan Ministry of Health schedule, as well as consultations with pediatric specialists. We accept young patients from birth.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: We accept cash (UZS), Visa and Mastercard bank cards, and online transfers via Click and Payme. A receipt is provided after every payment — you will need it for insurance reimbursement.

items.q: What are the clinic's working hours?
items.a: Our main reception hours are Monday to Saturday from 8:00 to 20:00, and Sunday from 9:00 to 15:00. Individual doctor schedules may differ — please confirm the exact appointment time when you book.
Our 24-hour emergency line operates every day — call us if you need an urgent consultation or a home visit. For life-threatening emergencies, please dial 103.

about.heading: About Shakh Clinics
about.body: We provide high-quality medical care for the whole family. Our clinic is equipped with modern technology and our doctors bring many years of experience. We care for every patient and strive to make treatment comfortable in a welcoming environment.

cta.heading: Take care of your health today
cta.body: Book a consultation now and receive a comprehensive examination with a 20% discount

meta.defaultTitle: Shakh Clinics — Private Medical Clinic in Moscow
meta.defaultDescription: Modern private clinic in Moscow, Russia. Experienced doctors, advanced equipment, affordable prices.

therapy-consultation.name: Therapist consultation
therapy-consultation.description: An initial appointment with a general practitioner at Shakh Clinics is the foundational step in assessing your overall health. During the consultation, the doctor will take a detailed history, perform a physical examination, and measure blood pressure, pulse, oxygen saturation, and temperature. Based on the findings, the therapist will order any necessary lab work or imaging studies and outline an individual plan for next steps. If a specialist consultation is needed, the doctor will refer you to the right colleague within the clinic. The appointment is suitable for adult patients with any symptoms, as well as for routine health assessments and medical certificates. Typical duration is around 30 minutes.

annual-checkup-comprehensive.name: Comprehensive annual check-up
annual-checkup-comprehensive.description: An extended medical check-up for those who take a proactive approach to their health. The program includes a therapist consultation, full blood and urine panels, biochemistry, ECG, ultrasound of the abdominal organs and thyroid gland, and measurement of glucose and lipid profiles. At the end of the assessment, the therapist consolidates all results into a single report, explains each marker, and provides personalized recommendations. The check-up is particularly valuable for patients over 35, those with hereditary risk factors, or anyone who hasn't seen a doctor in a long time. The full program takes about two hours and is completed in a single visit.

cardiology-consultation.name: Cardiologist consultation
cardiology-consultation.description: A cardiology appointment with a thorough review of cardiovascular complaints — shortness of breath, palpitations, elevated blood pressure, chest pain, swelling. The cardiologist will take a detailed history, perform auscultation of the heart and lungs, measure blood pressure on both arms, and assess cardiovascular risk using the SCORE and ASCVD scales. An ECG can be performed during the same visit if needed. The doctor will order additional studies (echocardiography, Holter monitoring, stress tests) and select an appropriate treatment plan. The consultation is suitable for both preventive visits and patients with established diagnoses.

cardiology-ecg.name: ECG with interpretation
cardiology-ecg.description: Electrocardiography is a quick, painless test that records the electrical activity of the heart over 5 to 10 minutes. The method allows us to detect rhythm abnormalities, signs of myocardial ischemia, chamber hypertrophy, and to evaluate the effect of antiarrhythmic therapy. The price already includes interpretation by a cardiologist with a written report and recommendations. Resting ECG is performed on the day of the visit without any preparation. The test is recommended annually after age 35, before any surgical procedure, and whenever cardiovascular symptoms appear.

cardiology-echo.name: Echocardiography (heart ultrasound)
cardiology-echo.description: Echocardiography is an ultrasound study of the heart that visualizes the chambers, valves, pericardium, and great vessels in real time. It helps assess myocardial contractility, measure ejection fraction, identify valve disease, cardiomyopathy, pulmonary hypertension, and post-infarction complications. The procedure is completely safe and painless, lasting about 30 to 45 minutes. No preparation is required. The report is provided on the same day with all key measurements and cardiologist recommendations. Suitable for adults and patients after cardiac surgery.

neurology-consultation.name: Neurologist consultation
neurology-consultation.description: A neurology appointment for the diagnosis and treatment of central and peripheral nervous system disorders. The neurologist treats headaches, dizziness, sleep disorders, neck and back pain, post-stroke conditions, neuralgias, and anxiety-depressive disorders with somatic manifestations. During the visit, the doctor performs a complete neurological exam: reflexes, muscle tone, sensation, coordination, and cognitive function. A plan for further investigation (MRI, EEG, lab tests) and treatment is then drawn up. The consultation lasts 45 minutes — enough time for a thorough discussion of each case.

neurology-eeg.name: Electroencephalography (EEG)
neurology-eeg.description: EEG is a painless and safe study of the brain's electrical activity. A cap fitted with sensors is placed on the patient's head and records cortical activity over 30 to 40 minutes. The test is used in epilepsy, sleep disorders, chronic headaches, after head trauma, and for diagnosing syncope of unclear origin. At our clinic, EEG is performed with functional stimuli — eye opening and closing, photic stimulation, and hyperventilation. The report is written by a neurologist describing all significant patterns and providing interpretation. Suitable for adults and children from age five.

gynecology-consultation.name: Gynecologist consultation
gynecology-consultation.description: A gynecology appointment with examination on the gynecological chair, sample collection for flora and cytology, and colposcopy when indicated. The consultation is suitable for routine preventive visits, menstrual cycle issues, pain, abnormal discharge, pregnancy planning, and contraception selection. The doctor will take a detailed reproductive history, perform an exam in a comfortable setting, and outline a plan for any further investigation. If changes are identified, we discuss treatment options and refer for pelvic ultrasound when needed. All procedures use single-use sterile instruments.

gynecology-ultrasound.name: Pelvic ultrasound
gynecology-ultrasound.description: Ultrasound of the uterus, ovaries, and adnexa is the cornerstone of gynecological diagnostics. We perform pelvic ultrasound in two ways: transabdominal (through the lower abdominal wall) and transvaginal (a more accurate method for assessing the endometrium and ovaries). The study can identify fibroids, cysts, endometriosis, endometrial pathology, pregnancy and its complications, and post-surgical changes. The procedure takes 20 to 30 minutes and requires no special preparation other than a moderately full bladder for the transabdominal approach. The report is provided immediately with detailed findings and recommendations.

urology-consultation.name: Urologist consultation
urology-consultation.description: A urology appointment for both men and women with disorders of the urinary and reproductive systems. The urologist treats cystitis, prostatitis, urolithiasis, urinary incontinence, erectile dysfunction, benign prostatic hyperplasia, and sexually transmitted infections. The doctor takes a thorough history, performs a discreet exam in a comfortable setting, and orders lab work as needed (urinalysis, cultures, PSA in men), as well as ultrasound of the bladder and prostate. At the end of the consultation, the doctor will propose a treatment plan — conservative or, when needed, surgical.

urology-uroflowmetry.name: Uroflowmetry
urology-uroflowmetry.description: Uroflowmetry is a functional test that objectively measures the speed and pattern of urination. The patient voids into a specialized device that automatically charts the flow. The study takes only a few minutes, requires no preparation aside from a moderately full bladder, and is completely painless. Uroflowmetry is essential when prostatic hyperplasia, neurogenic bladder, or urethral stricture is suspected, and for evaluating treatment response. The report is interpreted by a urologist with consideration of age, sex, and accompanying symptoms.

pediatrics-consultation.name: Pediatrician consultation
pediatrics-consultation.description: An appointment with an experienced pediatrician for children from birth to age 17. During the consultation, the doctor evaluates the child's physical and neuropsychological development, performs an examination, measures height and weight, and counsels parents on feeding, daily routine, vaccinations, and seasonal infection prevention. If the child is unwell, the pediatrician will make a diagnosis, order any necessary lab work, and prescribe age-appropriate treatment. The pediatrician's office is equipped with toys and materials that help children relax, and the doctor takes time to talk with parents without rushing.

pediatrics-vaccination.name: Children's vaccination
pediatrics-vaccination.description: Routine childhood vaccination according to the National Immunization Schedule of Uzbekistan and individual schedules. Before each immunization, the pediatrician examines the child, checks temperature, and assesses overall health to rule out contraindications. The clinic uses certified vaccines from well-known manufacturers, stored in compliance with cold-chain requirements. After the vaccination, the child is observed for 30 minutes to detect any immediate reactions. The cost depends on the chosen product — the doctor discusses all options with parents in advance and helps select the most suitable one.

dermatology-consultation.name: Dermatologist consultation
dermatology-consultation.description: A dermatology appointment for the diagnosis and treatment of skin, hair, and nail conditions. The dermatologist manages acne, eczema, psoriasis, fungal infections, allergic dermatitis, hair loss, moles, and other lesions. During the exam, the doctor inspects affected areas with a dermatoscope — this allows magnified visualization of the skin's structure and more accurate diagnosis. If a malignant process is suspected, the patient is referred for urgent biopsy. Based on the findings, an individualized treatment plan is selected, taking into account skin type, lifestyle, and any comorbid conditions.

dermatology-laser.name: Laser procedure
dermatology-laser.description: Laser treatment of skin conditions at our clinic is performed on modern equipment by certified specialists. The list of procedures includes removal of vascular networks and capillaries, treatment of pigmented spots, resurfacing of post-acne scars, laser hair removal, and removal of papillomas and benign skin lesions. A dermatologist consultation with assessment of skin type and contraindications is mandatory before the procedure. Sessions last 30 to 60 minutes depending on the area treated. Detailed aftercare instructions are provided: sun protection, moisturization, and activity restrictions. Pricing depends on the size of the treatment area and the number of sessions required.

ophthalmology-consultation.name: Ophthalmologist consultation
ophthalmology-consultation.description: A comprehensive appointment with an ophthalmologist including visual acuity testing, intraocular pressure measurement, and fundus examination using a Goldmann lens. The consultation is suitable for vision deterioration, headaches, eye pain, redness, watering, or a foreign-body sensation. We see adults and children from age three. If your child complains of fatigue while reading or struggles to see the school whiteboard — that's a good reason to schedule a preventive exam. Based on the findings, the doctor will write a prescription for glasses or contact lenses, or outline a treatment plan for any condition identified.

ophthalmology-vision-test.name: Visual acuity test
ophthalmology-vision-test.description: Basic visometry with refraction — determination of visual acuity for each eye using a Sivtsev chart, with correction trials using lenses of varying power to identify the appropriate prescription. The price includes a written prescription for glasses or contact lenses. The test takes 20 to 25 minutes, requires no preparation, is completely painless, and is suitable for children from age five and adults of any age. If the ophthalmologist notices changes during the test, additional studies (tonometry, fundus examination) will be offered. We recommend a yearly vision check.

dental-cleaning.name: Professional teeth cleaning
dental-cleaning.description: Comprehensive oral hygiene in three stages: ultrasonic removal of dental calculus, plaque removal with the Air Flow system using a specialty powder, and a finishing polish with paste and fluoridation. The procedure removes hard deposits and pigment stains from tea, coffee, and tobacco, restores the natural color of the enamel, and reduces the risk of caries and gum disease. The dental hygienist uses gentle techniques — the procedure is virtually painless. After the appointment, the patient receives recommendations on home care, including the choice of brush, paste, and irrigator. Cleaning is recommended every six months.

dental-implant.name: Dental implantation
dental-implant.description: Placement of a dental implant is a modern way to restore a missing tooth without grinding down adjacent teeth. The clinic works with implant systems from trusted global manufacturers (Switzerland, South Korea, Israel) — the specific brand is discussed with the patient at the consultation. The process includes initial assessment, planning based on a CT scan, surgical placement of the implant under local anesthesia, an osseointegration period (3 to 6 months), and finally placement of the crown. At each stage, the patient receives detailed care instructions. Pricing varies depending on the implant system and the complexity of the clinical situation.

dental-whitening.name: Teeth whitening
dental-whitening.description: Professional in-office teeth whitening using modern gels activated by an LED lamp. The procedure takes one visit of about 60 minutes and lightens enamel by 4 to 8 shades. Professional cleaning and a dental exam are required prior to whitening — this is essential to assess enamel condition, rule out caries, and select the appropriate gel concentration. The procedure has contraindications (pregnancy, marked tooth sensitivity, untreated caries), making the consultation a mandatory step. After whitening, we recommend a 48-hour "white diet": no coffee, tea, red wine, or berries.

endocrinology-consultation.name: Endocrinologist consultation
endocrinology-consultation.description: An endocrinology appointment for the diagnosis and treatment of thyroid disease, diabetes mellitus, obesity, hormonal menstrual irregularities, and osteoporosis. The endocrinologist takes a detailed history, performs an examination and palpation of the thyroid, and assesses body mass index and fat distribution. Based on the consultation, the doctor will order a complete hormonal panel (TSH, T4, T3, cortisol, insulin, OGTT) and imaging studies (thyroid ultrasound, densitometry). Once results are available, an individualized treatment and follow-up plan is established.

endocrinology-thyroid-ultrasound.name: Thyroid ultrasound
endocrinology-thyroid-ultrasound.description: Ultrasound of the thyroid gland is the primary imaging method for this organ. It allows assessment of lobe and isthmus dimensions, detection of nodules, cysts, signs of inflammation, and structural changes. The procedure takes 20 to 30 minutes, is completely safe, and requires no special preparation. The doctor provides a written report measuring gland volume and describing each nodule using the TIRADS system. If suspicious lesions are found, the endocrinologist will refer the patient for fine-needle aspiration biopsy to clarify the nature of the nodule. Ultrasound is recommended for any neck symptoms and when hormonal imbalance is suspected.

gastro-consultation.name: Gastroenterologist consultation
gastro-consultation.description: A gastroenterology appointment with a detailed history and abdominal examination. The gastroenterologist treats gastritis, peptic ulcer disease, reflux esophagitis, irritable bowel syndrome, hepatobiliary disorders, pancreatitis, and dysbiosis. If the patient already has the results of a gastroscopy, abdominal ultrasound, or biochemistry, the doctor integrates them into the treatment plan. The visit produces an individual diet plan, medication regimen, and follow-up schedule. Suitable for first-time visitors and for patients in long-term follow-up.

gastro-fgds.name: Gastroscopy (EGD)
gastro-fgds.description: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy is an endoscopic examination of the esophagus, stomach, and duodenum. A thin flexible endoscope with a camera is inserted through the mouth, allowing inspection of the mucosa from inside. The procedure detects gastritis, ulcers, tumors, esophageal varices, and reflux esophagitis. When indicated, biopsy and Helicobacter pylori testing are performed. At our clinic, gastroscopy is done with local anesthesia and, at the patient's request, with sedation. Preparation is straightforward: no food or drink for 8 hours before the procedure. After the procedure, the patient receives photo or video material along with a written report.

orthopedics-consultation.name: Orthopedic and trauma consultation
orthopedics-consultation.description: An orthopedic appointment for the diagnosis of musculoskeletal disorders and injuries. The orthopedist treats osteochondrosis, intervertebral disc herniation, osteoarthritis, post-traumatic conditions, flat feet, postural disorders in children, and foot deformities. During the visit, the doctor performs an orthopedic exam assessing joint range of motion, muscle strength, and neurological status. Imaging (X-ray, MRI, or CT) is ordered when needed to clarify the diagnosis. Suitable for athletes, office workers with chronic back pain, elderly patients with arthritis, and parents wanting an assessment of their child's posture and feet.

orthopedics-joint-injection.name: Intra-articular injection
orthopedics-joint-injection.description: An intra-articular injection is an effective way to relieve inflammation and pain in arthritis, bursitis, and tendinitis. Depending on indications, corticosteroids, hyaluronic acid, or platelet-rich plasma may be used. The procedure is performed by an orthopedist under sterile conditions, using anatomical landmarks or ultrasound guidance to ensure accurate needle placement. The procedure takes 30 to 45 minutes. After the injection, joint loading should be limited for 24 hours. The therapeutic effect develops gradually over 5 to 10 days. A course of 3 to 5 injections every 7 to 14 days is typically required.

diagnostics-mri.name: MRI scan
diagnostics-mri.description: Magnetic resonance imaging is a high-precision method for visualizing soft tissues: brain and spinal cord, joints, internal organs, and blood vessels. MRI does not use ionizing radiation, making it safe for most patients. The scan takes 30 to 60 minutes depending on the area examined. The patient lies still inside the scanner; for those with claustrophobia, mild sedation is possible by prior arrangement with the doctor. Reports are produced by a radiologist with detailed findings and comparison with prior studies when available. Preparation depends on the area of the body — details are provided when booking.

diagnostics-blood-panel-full.name: Comprehensive blood panel
diagnostics-blood-panel-full.description: A comprehensive lab profile to assess overall health. The package includes a complete blood count with differential and ESR, biochemistry (glucose, total protein, bilirubin, ALT, AST, creatinine, urea, cholesterol and lipid profile), iron metabolism markers, and TSH. Blood is drawn in the morning on an empty stomach by a qualified nurse in comfortable conditions. Results are ready within one business day and available by email or in your personal account. Interpretation by a therapist is included on request — the doctor will walk you through each marker and advise whether further investigation is needed.

zaripov-shakhzod-zhurakulovich.name: Zaripov Shakhzod Zhurakulovich
zaripov-shakhzod-zhurakulovich.education: Completed specialized dental training and regularly advances his skills in prosthetic and surgical dentistry.
zaripov-shakhzod-zhurakulovich.achievements: Focuses on restoring dentition, surgical preparation for prosthetics, and comprehensive treatment planning.
zaripov-shakhzod-zhurakulovich.bio: Shakhzod Zhurakulovich is an orthopedic dentist and oral surgeon with more than 7 years of experience. He helps patients restore function and smile aesthetics with treatment plans tailored to the clinical case, timing, and comfort. His work emphasizes precise diagnostics, careful surgical tactics, and clear explanation of each treatment stage.

movsumov-mesud-nizamievich.name: Movsumov Mesud Nizamievich
movsumov-mesud-nizamievich.education: Completed dental training and continues additional education in modern therapeutic dentistry.
movsumov-mesud-nizamievich.achievements: Works with caries treatment, complications, aesthetic restorations, and oral disease prevention.
movsumov-mesud-nizamievich.bio: Mesud Nizamievich is a general dentist with more than 4 years of experience. He conducts initial consultations, prepares clear treatment plans, and helps patients preserve their natural teeth as gently as possible. During appointments, he explains the diagnosis, treatment stages, and home-care recommendations in detail.

salpagarova-larisa-khadzhimuradovna.name: Salpagarova Larisa Khadzhimuradovna
salpagarova-larisa-khadzhimuradovna.education: Completed dental training and develops her expertise in therapeutic dentistry and prevention.
salpagarova-larisa-khadzhimuradovna.achievements: Specializes in therapeutic treatment, tooth restoration, and preventive patient care.
salpagarova-larisa-khadzhimuradovna.bio: Larisa Khadzhimuradovna is a general dentist with more than 5 years of experience. She carefully performs caries treatment, restorations, and preventive procedures. Her approach emphasizes calm communication, attentive examination, and comfortable treatment without unnecessary haste.

clinic-opens-new-pediatric-wing.title: Shakh Clinics opens its renovated pediatric wing
clinic-opens-new-pediatric-wing.excerpt: Starting April 12, the clinic's fully renovated pediatric wing opened with a spacious play area and three pediatrician offices.
clinic-opens-new-pediatric-wing.body:
We are pleased to announce that the fully renovated pediatric wing of Shakh Clinics opened this April. The clinic's second floor now hosts three spacious pediatrician offices, a separate procedure room for vaccinations and blood draws, and a large play area for our young patients with books and educational toys.

When designing the wing, we took the input of parents and pediatricians into account. The offices are decorated in calm pastel tones and equipped with modern stadiometers and scales for children of all ages, and every appointment offers a dedicated changing table for our smallest patients. Sound insulation in the rooms means parents with infants don't have to worry about keeping things quiet during exams.

Appointments in the new wing are run by experienced pediatricians, including Yuldasheva Dilnoza Bahtiyorovna with 17 years of clinical experience. The team also welcomes pediatric ophthalmologist Mirzayev Jasur Saidovich and a visiting pediatric neurologist. This means parents can complete all the main check-ups in one place, without travelling between clinics.

We have also introduced a "Children's Health Day" service — a complete pediatric assessment in a single visit, including a pediatrician consultation, examinations by specialists, lab work, and abdominal ultrasound. The service is especially popular before the start of the school year and ahead of nursery enrollment.

Bookings for the new wing are now open. The pediatricians' schedules can be confirmed with the receptionists or a convenient slot can be reserved through the form on our website.

free-cardio-screening-day.title: Free cardio screening day — March 28
free-cardio-screening-day.excerpt: As part of Heart Health Month, the clinic ran a free diagnostic day — over 200 people attended.
free-cardio-screening-day.body:
On Saturday, March 28, Shakh Clinics held a Free Cardiac Screening Day. Anyone could walk in without an appointment or fee for a basic cardiovascular check — blood pressure measurement, a resting ECG, and a brief cardiologist consultation with interpretation of the results.

More than 200 people were screened over the course of the day. A significant portion of visitors had previously undiagnosed conditions — elevated blood pressure or specific arrhythmias requiring further investigation. All such patients received a free follow-up appointment with our cardiologists for a more detailed evaluation, including Holter monitoring or echocardiography.

Karimov Sherzod Alisherovich, top-category cardiologist and the event's coordinator, noted: "Many people live with high blood pressure for years without knowing it. Early detection of hypertension and choosing the right therapy is a direct path to reducing the risk of stroke and heart attack down the line." He added that the clinic plans to make these screening days a regular event — the next is scheduled for the autumn.

Among our other initiatives is an educational program for patients with established ischemic heart disease. The clinic is ready to teach patients, free of charge, how to monitor their own blood pressure and pulse, the proper technique for taking medications, and how to act during a hypertensive crisis.

Follow our social media for upcoming announcements — the next free initiative will focus on endocrine disease and will take place on World Diabetes Day.

winter-flu-prevention-tips.title: How to protect yourself from the flu this winter
winter-flu-prevention-tips.excerpt: Winter is peak season for viral respiratory infections. Shakh Clinics's general practitioner shares evidence-based ways to prevent flu and other respiratory illnesses.
winter-flu-prevention-tips.body:
Winter is traditionally accompanied by a rise in cases of influenza and other acute respiratory viral infections. Despite Tashkent's relatively mild winters, the risk of widespread outbreaks remains high — especially in public places, transport, schools, and offices.

The primary specific prevention method is annual vaccination. Modern flu vaccines cover four currently circulating virus strains and provide protection two to three weeks after the shot. Vaccination is particularly recommended for children, adults over 65, pregnant women, and patients with chronic conditions such as diabetes, COPD, and heart failure. At our clinic, vaccinations use certified imported preparations and are always preceded by a mandatory examination by a general practitioner.

Non-specific protective measures matter just as much. These include regular hand washing with soap, hand sanitizer after touching surfaces in public spaces, ventilating rooms several times a day, and maintaining comfortable indoor humidity. At the first symptoms of a respiratory infection — runny nose, cough, sore throat, fever — it's best to stay home and avoid public places to prevent spreading the virus.

Antiviral medications are a separate topic. Most heavily advertised products lack proven efficacy. Genuine benefit during the flu comes only from neuraminidase inhibitors, and only when started within the first 48 hours of symptoms. These should be prescribed by a doctor — self-prescription is not justified.

If your fever exceeds 38.5 °C for more than three days, or if you develop shortness of breath, chest pain, or severe weakness, seek medical care immediately. These can be signs of complications that require active treatment.

dental-implant-technology-update.title: New technologies in dental implantation
dental-implant-technology-update.excerpt: Shakh Clinics has implemented digital implant planning with CT imaging and 3D modelling for greater accuracy.
dental-implant-technology-update.body:
In February, the dental team at Shakh Clinics completed the rollout of a new digital protocol for dental implantation. Implant placement is now planned using CT imaging of the jaw and three-dimensional surgical modelling on specialized software.

Before the procedure, we obtain a detailed CT scan of the implant zone, which is used to build a virtual model of the patient's jaw. The implant surgeon can determine the optimal position, angle, and depth of each implant in advance, taking into account the location of nerves, maxillary sinuses, and vascular structures. From the virtual plan, an individualized surgical guide can be fabricated — fitted onto the jaw during the procedure, it directs the instrument along the precise planned trajectory.

This approach offers several important advantages. The risk of damage to the inferior alveolar nerve and other anatomical structures is reduced, implant stability in bone is improved, and the surgical trauma is minimized. For the patient, this means faster healing, less swelling after the procedure, and fewer complications.

Usmanov Ravshan Makhmudovich, the clinic's leading implantologist, has completed dedicated training on the new system and has already used it in more than fifty cases over the past month. According to him, implant positioning accuracy has improved substantially compared with the classical technique.

Digital planning is especially valuable in complex cases — implant placement in zones with insufficient bone, All-on-4 and All-on-6 protocols, and sinus lifting. At the free initial consultation, we determine how much your case will benefit from digital planning and propose the optimal treatment plan.

women-health-week-2026.title: Women's Health Week at Shakh Clinics
women-health-week-2026.excerpt: From March 8 to 14 the clinic is running a special initiative for women — comprehensive screening with discounts on ultrasound and lab work.
women-health-week-2026.body:
To mark International Women's Day, Shakh Clinics is announcing Women's Health Week from March 8 to 14. Throughout the week, women can undergo a comprehensive gynecological screening on special terms.

The program includes: an obstetrician-gynecologist consultation with examination on the gynecological chair, sample collection for flora and oncocytology, pelvic ultrasound, a mammologist consultation, and breast ultrasound. An extended hormonal panel is also available at a special price for women planning pregnancy or in perimenopause.

The clinic's lead gynecologist Tursunova Malika Shavkatovna reminds us: "Regular gynecological screening is the best form of prevention. The majority of serious conditions of the female reproductive system can be caught at an early, fully treatable stage. Cytological testing in particular can reveal pre-cancerous changes in the cervix years before they develop into cancer."

This year we are placing particular emphasis on the reproductive health of younger women — from adolescence through pregnancy planning. Malika Shavkatovna and her colleagues will run a series of free educational talks on Saturday and Sunday — registration is open through the clinic's reception.

For women over 40 the program additionally includes bone densitometry and an endocrinology consultation on hormonal support during perimenopause. All procedures take place in our women-only consulting rooms in the most comfortable setting possible.

Registration for Women's Health Week is now open — secure your preferred slot early.

new-mri-machine-installation.title: A new MRI scanner has been installed at the clinic
new-mri-machine-installation.excerpt: In December a modern MRI scanner with improved image quality went online in Shakh Clinics's diagnostic department.
new-mri-machine-installation.body:
In December, Shakh Clinics's diagnostic department completed the installation and commissioning of a new 1.5 Tesla MRI scanner. The new system replaces previous-generation equipment and significantly broadens our diagnostic capabilities.

The new scanner produces higher-quality images and shortens scan times — a standard brain MRI or single-region spine study now takes 25 to 30 minutes instead of the previous 45. This is especially important for patients with claustrophobia or those who find it difficult to remain still for long periods. The bore of the scanner is wider and shorter, noticeably improving comfort during the exam.

The scanner enables specialized MRI protocols — diffusion-weighted imaging for the early diagnosis of stroke, MR cholangiopancreatography without contrast, and MR angiography for assessment of brain and neck vessels. Joint imaging with high cartilage detail is also available, which is critical for diagnosing early-stage osteoarthritis and sports injuries.

All studies are reported by radiologist Azimova Shahnoza Davronovna, who has 12 years of clinical experience. The report is delivered the same day; in complex cases or where an urgent result is needed, the report is provided within two hours of the scan.

MRI appointments can be booked through the website or by calling the clinic. Before the scan, please remove all metal items and inform us about any implants, pacemakers, or tattoos — these may affect whether the procedure can be performed.

vaccination-schedule-children-2026.title: Children's vaccination schedule for 2026
vaccination-schedule-children-2026.excerpt: Our pediatrician has prepared an updated routine vaccination calendar with notes for each age stage.
vaccination-schedule-children-2026.body:
With the new year, many parents wonder which vaccines a child needs and at what age. Shakh Clinics pediatrician Yuldasheva Dilnoza Bahtiyorovna has prepared an updated practical immunization calendar based on Uzbekistan's National Immunization Schedule and World Health Organization recommendations.

Within the first 12 hours of life, the newborn receives a hepatitis B vaccine. Between days 3 and 7, the BCG vaccine for tuberculosis is given. By two months of age, the child receives the first doses of vaccines against polio, diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, and Haemophilus influenzae type B. At six months, the second dose of hepatitis B and the third dose of polio are added.

At one year of age, vaccines for measles, rubella, and mumps are administered. At 18 months — the first booster against diphtheria, tetanus, pertussis, and polio. At six years — the second booster. At 14 years, children receive a booster against diphtheria and tetanus, as well as the second BCG dose.

In addition to the calendar vaccines, we recommend further vaccines with proven efficacy: yearly influenza vaccination from six months of age, varicella vaccine at one year, rotavirus vaccine starting at two months, pneumococcal vaccine, and human papillomavirus vaccine for adolescents from age 9.

Before every vaccination at our clinic, the pediatrician performs a mandatory examination assessing the child's general condition, taking the temperature, and reviewing recent history. After the shot, the child stays under the doctor's observation for 30 minutes to rule out the rare immediate reactions. All vaccines we use are quality-certified and stored under strict temperature control.

diabetes-awareness-month.title: November — Diabetes Awareness Month
diabetes-awareness-month.excerpt: Our endocrinologist talks about the warning signs of diabetes and the importance of early detection.
diabetes-awareness-month.body:
November is observed worldwide as a month dedicated to combating diabetes mellitus — a condition affecting millions and often going unnoticed for years. Shakh Clinics endocrinologist Nuriddinova Nigora Shavkatovna reminds us of the warning signs and the importance of timely screening.

Type 2 diabetes develops gradually and may produce no clear symptoms for many years. By the time of diagnosis, many patients already have early complications — kidney involvement, retinal changes, or peripheral nerve damage. That's why preventive blood-sugar testing is essential for all adults, especially those with overweight, a sedentary lifestyle, a family history of diabetes, or women who had gestational diabetes during pregnancy.

Warning signs to watch for: persistent thirst, frequent and copious urination, increased fatigue, slow healing of small cuts and wounds, itchy skin (especially in the genital area), reduced visual acuity, and numbness or tingling in the feet. If any of these appear, we recommend testing fasting glucose and glycated hemoglobin (HbA1c).

During this awareness month, the clinic is running a special program: for all new patients with suspected diabetes, an initial endocrinology consultation including blood glucose, HbA1c, and basic biochemistry is offered on preferential terms. After diagnosis, we help patients build a complete plan — from selecting therapy to learning self-monitoring of blood sugar.

We place particular emphasis on patient education. Patients with newly diagnosed diabetes receive an individual session with the endocrinologist and nurse covering glucometer technique, insulin therapy rules (where indicated), carbohydrate counting, and dietary principles. Knowing these basics is half the battle in long-term management of the disease.

eye-health-screen-time-tips.title: Eye health in the age of screens
eye-health-screen-time-tips.excerpt: Our ophthalmologist shares practical tips for protecting your vision when you spend a lot of time on computers and smartphones.
eye-health-screen-time-tips.body:
The modern person spends a significant portion of the day looking at the screens of computers, tablets, and smartphones. This places a new kind of strain on our visual system, one that evolution did not prepare us for. The result is computer vision syndrome, which manifests as eye dryness and irritation, headaches, blurred vision, and general fatigue by the end of the day.

Shakh Clinics ophthalmologist Mirzayev Jasur Saidovich reminds us of the simple but effective "20-20-20" rule: every twenty minutes of screen work, take a twenty-second break and look at an object about six metres away. This shifts focus and relaxes the eye muscles. Throughout the working day, try to take full five-minute breaks every hour — get up, step away from your desk, and look out the window into the distance.

The physical setup matters too. Position your monitor so that its centre is at or just below eye level, about an arm's length away. Lighting in the room should be soft and produce no glare on the screen. Adjust contrast and brightness so that the screen does not appear excessively bright relative to the surrounding environment. Modern devices offer an "eye protection" mode that reduces blue light — particularly useful in the evening.

If your eyes often feel dry, try preservative-free moisturizing drops. Blinking frequency drops several times when working at a screen, so the tear film evaporates faster than usual. A small humidifier near your desk also helps — especially in winter, when central heating dries out the air.

For children and teenagers it is especially important to limit screen time and ensure plenty of time outdoors. Daylight walks, as modern research shows, slow the progression of myopia at this age. And do schedule a yearly preventive ophthalmology exam — even without symptoms, many issues are only revealed by instrumental testing.

dermatologist-acne-treatment-guide.title: A modern approach to acne treatment
dermatologist-acne-treatment-guide.excerpt: Our dermatologist debunks myths about acne and explains evidence-based ways to manage the condition.
dermatologist-acne-treatment-guide.body:
Acne is one of the most common dermatological conditions, affecting not only teenagers but adults as well. Despite the topic's popularity on social media, real acne treatment is grounded in evidence-based medicine, not in advice from influencers. Shakh Clinics dermatologist Rakhimova Zuhra Azizovna shares a professional perspective on the problem.

The first and most important myth is that acne can be cured simply by washing more often or using more aggressive products. In fact, excessive washing damages the skin's natural protective barrier and can worsen inflammation. The modern approach is gentle cleansing once or twice a day with a mild sulfate-free cleanser and regular moisturization with light, oil-free creams.

The second misconception is that adult acne calls for the same products as teenage acne. Not so. Adult acne, particularly in women, is often hormonally driven and requires a comprehensive approach — assessment of hormonal status, sometimes a referral to an endocrinologist, and an individualized regimen of topical and systemic treatments.

Evidence-based methods for treating acne include topical retinoids (adapalene, tretinoin), topical antibiotics combined with benzoyl peroxide, azelaic acid, and for moderate-to-severe disease, courses of systemic antibiotics or systemic retinoids (isotretinoin). The latter are used under strict indications and under dermatologist supervision with regular lab monitoring.

It's important to understand that treating acne is always a long process. Visible improvements typically appear after 4 to 8 weeks of consistent use of the chosen regimen. Stopping treatment at the first sign of improvement is not a good idea — this often leads to relapse. Post-acne scarring and pigmentation, once the inflammatory process is stable, can be effectively treated with laser procedures and mesotherapy. So if your skin is troubling you, don't self-treat — book a dermatology consultation.

family-medicine-package-launch.title: Launch of the "Family Medicine" package
family-medicine-package-launch.excerpt: The clinic introduces a year-long family medical-care program with a fixed price and an extended set of services.
family-medicine-package-launch.body:
In February we are launching a new program — the "Family Medicine" package, designed for year-round medical care of an entire family. The program was developed in response to requests from patients who have been managing their health at Shakh Clinics for several years and would like a simpler way to interact with the clinic.

The package covers unlimited consultations with the family general practitioner for one year, two preventive examinations per year for every member of the family with basic lab work and imaging studies, consultations with the clinic's specialists (pediatrician, cardiologist, endocrinologist, gynecologist) when medically indicated at no additional charge, an emergency telephone consultation with the family doctor at any time of day, and routine childhood vaccinations.

Each family is assigned a personal family doctor — meaning a single specialist knows the medical history of all family members, sees the shared hereditary risks, and can offer a coordinated plan. General practitioner Qosimov Akmal Tolkinovich, who is coordinating the program, notes: "Family medicine is not about treating symptoms — it's about preserving the long-term health of the whole family. When I know the parents' history, I better understand the child's risks, and vice versa."

The package is particularly suited to families with young children, elderly relatives, or patients with chronic conditions requiring regular follow-up. The price is calculated individually based on family composition and the number of members. At the first meeting, the family doctor performs a comprehensive examination of every participant and prepares a yearly preventive-care plan.

For those not yet ready for an annual package, we continue to offer pay-per-service options under our standard price list. Registration for a free orientation meeting and price calculation is open — please contact the clinic's receptionist or use the form on our website.

partnership-with-tashkent-medical-academ.title: Partnership with the Tashkent Medical Academy
partnership-with-tashkent-medical-academ.excerpt: The clinic has signed a scientific and clinical cooperation agreement with one of the country's leading medical universities.
partnership-with-tashkent-medical-academ.body:
In December, Shakh Clinics signed an agreement on scientific and clinical cooperation with the Tashkent Medical Academy — one of Uzbekistan's leading medical universities. The partnership covers several directions and aims to improve the quality of medical care and to support the training of the next generation of doctors.

Under the agreement, the clinic becomes a teaching site for senior students and resident physicians from the academy, working under the supervision of our experienced specialists. This means young doctors gain the opportunity to work in a modern clinical environment using up-to-date protocols and equipment. For our part, we have a meaningful chance to participate in training the country's healthcare workforce.

A separate strand is joint scientific work. We plan to take part in clinical studies run by the academy, share de-identified data for epidemiological research, and publish joint scientific papers. Neurologist Nazarov Alisher Rustamovich, PhD, will serve as Shakh Clinics's coordinator for the scientific program.

In the educational direction, joint seminars and roundtables for practicing city doctors are planned. Topics for the first events are already being discussed — modern approaches to managing patients with metabolic syndrome, new antihypertensive guidelines, and updates in pediatric vaccination. The seminars will be open to colleagues from other clinics and to primary-care physicians.

For our patients, this partnership means access to consultations with the academy's leading specialists in difficult clinical cases. If a situation calls for the opinion of an academic expert, we can quickly arrange a consultation with professors from the relevant departments without administrative red tape. The agreement enters into force on January 1 and is set for five years, with an option to extend.

r-001.text: I saw Karimov Sherzod Alisherovich about high blood pressure. A very attentive doctor — he asked detailed questions, took my pressure on both arms, and did an ECG right there in the appointment. He explained which tests I needed to take and why. He prescribed treatment, and after two weeks of follow-up my pressure stabilized. Many thanks to the clinic for having such a competent cardiologist.

r-002.text: We've been seeing Dilnoza Bahtiyorovna since my daughter was born — she's two now. The doctor is wonderful — patient, attentive, and great with kids. When my daughter caught a cold, she explained everything in detail, wrote out the treatment plan, and called the next day to check how she was doing. That kind of follow-up is rare for a private clinic.

r-003.text: I had my teeth cleaned by Kasymova Feruza Tahirovna. The procedure was comfortable, with no discomfort. The doctor told me which products to use at home and showed me proper flossing technique. The only minor thing — I had to wait a bit because the previous appointment ran over. Overall a good experience.

r-004.text: I had a pelvic ultrasound with Tursunova Malika Shavkatovna. The doctor is very tactful and professional, she explained everything thoroughly, showed it on the screen, and described what each thing was. The report was given immediately. The atmosphere in the room is calm — no feeling of being on a medical conveyor belt. I recommend the clinic to women looking for a gynecologist.

r-005.text: I had been complaining of constant headaches for over six months. I booked with neurologist Nazarov Alisher Rustamovich. The doctor didn't order a pile of unnecessary tests like the previous clinic — he did a thorough exam, asked about lifestyle and work hours. He diagnosed me with tension-type migraine and selected treatment. Two weeks into the medications, my condition is significantly better.

r-006.text: I booked with ophthalmologist Mirzayev Jasur Saidovich because my eyes were getting worse after long hours at the computer. He did a complete exam — visual acuity, intraocular pressure, fundus check. He explained computer vision syndrome and gave me practical tips on work routine and eye exercises. He prescribed glasses — I wear them with pleasure, they really helped.

r-007.text: I went to dermatologist Rakhimova Zuhra Azizovna with problem skin on my face. She did a careful exam, performed dermatoscopy of my moles, and laid out a regimen with topical care and systemic medications. Two months later my skin is much better. The only thing — at first I was a bit thrown off by how young the doctor looked, but her experience and approach won me over.

r-008.text: I had an echocardiogram with cardiologist Karimov Sherzod Alisherovich on referral from my district doctor. The exam took less than an hour and was very comfortable. He showed me the heart chambers on the screen, explained every measurement, and wrote out the report point by point. He also adjusted the treatment plan I had been on previously. Very satisfied with the visit.

r-009.text: I had two implants placed by surgeon Usmanov Ravshan Makhmudovich. At the consultation everything was clearly laid out — stages, timing, cost, possible risks. The surgery itself was done under local anesthesia, no pain. Crowns were placed four months later. A year has passed — no problems at all, I eat and smile without worry. Thank you to the doctor for his work.

r-010.text: I went to endocrinologist Nuriddinova Nigora Shavkatovna complaining of constant fatigue. I did the labs she ordered, and it turned out my thyroid was the issue. She did the ultrasound right there in the office and prescribed treatment. Two months on the medication and I feel significantly better. Very grateful for her attentive approach and accurate diagnosis.

r-011.text: I had a gastroscopy with gastroenterologist Tashpulatov Shokhrukh Bakhtiyorovich. I was very worried before the procedure, but the doctor calmly explained every step. We did it with sedation — I fell asleep, and a minute later I was awake. After the exam he showed me the images and walked me through what was what. The procedure was unpleasant but quick and without aftereffects.

r-012.text: I saw urologist Ismailov Bobur Uktamovich. A tactful, knowledgeable doctor — there was no awkwardness during the appointment. He made a diagnosis, prescribed treatment. Three weeks later at follow-up everything was significantly better. You can book online, very convenient. The clinic is clean, the staff are polite — overall a pleasant impression.

r-013.text: I had a comprehensive annual check-up with general practitioner Khodjayeva Gulchehra Rustamovna. A very experienced and attentive doctor — I'm 62, and for the first time in many years she went through all my medications and optimized the regimen. I used to take eight, now only five, and I feel better. Huge thanks for treating the patient as a person.

r-014.text: I had an intra-articular injection in my knee with orthopedist Pulatov Sardor Anvarovich. He did the exam, explained why hyaluronic acid was right for my case, and did the injection quickly and accurately under ultrasound guidance. By the third day after the injection the pain was almost gone. I'm scheduled for the next session in two weeks. Very pleased with the result.

r-015.text: I had a spine MRI on referral from a neurologist. Booking was quick, and I got the report the same day. The scanner itself is modern, and the bore was less cramped than at other clinics I've been to. The only thing — I had to wait about twenty minutes past my appointment time. Dr. Azimova produced the report, very detailed.

r-016.text: I booked with general practitioner Qosimov Akmal Tolkinovich for a basic cold. The doctor is young but very switched-on — no useless filler medications, clear recommendations, justified prescriptions. A week later it had cleared up. I'm signing up with him as my family GP. It's convenient that he works evenings — I can fit him in after work.

r-017.text: I had teeth whitening with Kasymova Feruza Tahirovna. Before the procedure I went through a consultation and a cleaning. The procedure itself took about an hour, and it was a bit uncomfortable due to tooth sensitivity, but the doctor took breaks and checked in with me. My teeth lightened by several shades and the effect has held for several months now. The price matches the quality.

r-018.text: We had my daughter's routine vaccination with pediatrician Yuldasheva Dilnoza Bahtiyorovna. Before the shot, the doctor examined her, took her temperature, and asked how she'd been over the past week. My daughter cried a little, but the doctor calmed her down quickly. After the shot they asked us to wait half an hour in the corridor under observation. Everything to a high standard.

r-019.text: I had an ECG with cardiologist Karimov Sherzod Alisherovich before a business trip. Quick, no queue, the doctor interpreted it on the spot and gave me the report. He noticed minor changes and recommended a Holter as a follow-up — I'm booked for next week. Thanks for the responsiveness and attention to detail.

r-020.text: My wife has been seeing gynecologist Tursunova Malika Shavkatovna for several years now. At her last preventive visit the doctor caught small changes that nobody had noticed before. She ordered additional tests and explained in detail what needed to be done. We are very grateful to the clinic and the doctor for the attentive approach and professionalism.

intro.heading: About Shakh Clinics
intro.subtitle: Modern medicine in a comfortable and safe environment
paragraphs: Shakh Clinics is a modern private clinic in Moscow where care for your health goes hand in hand with comfort and attention to every patient. We have brought together experienced doctors, advanced diagnostic and treatment technologies, and thoughtfully designed spaces to make every visit calm and convenient.
Our team works to international quality standards and continually develops its expertise. We believe that a patient's trust begins with honest communication, accurate diagnosis and a human approach.
